author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
vibhāvita⋅rati jabe, kriyāpara ha’ye tabe,
anubhāva haya ta’ udita
citta-bhāva udghāṭiyā, kare bāhya suvikriyā,
jakhana je haya ta’ ucita

nṛtya, gīta, viluṇṭhana, krośana, tanu-moṭana,
huṅkāra, jṛmbhaṇa, ghanaśvāsa
lokānapekṣitā mati, lālāsrāva, ghūrṇā ati,
hikkādaya, aṭṭa aṭṭa hāsa

gātra⋅citta⋅jāta saba, ‘alaṅkāra’ suvaibhava,
nigadita viṁśati prakāra
‘udbhāsvara’ nāma tāra, dhammilla⋅sraṁsana āra,
phullaghrāṇa, nīvyādi vikāra

vilāpālāpa, saŋlāpa, pralāpa o anulāpa,
apalāpa, sandeśātideśa
apadeśa, upadeśa, nirdeśa o vyapadeśa,
‘vācika’ ānubhāvera viśeṣa
en_translation:
When awakened love becomes active, then external manifestations of emotion arise. Revealing the inner emotions, it creates excellent external transformations, whatever is appropriate at that time.

Dancing, singing, rolling on the ground, crying loudly, twisting the body, roaring, yawning, heavy breathing, disregard for public opinion, salivation, excessive reeling, hiccups and so on, and loud laughter.

All born from the body and mind, these 'ornaments' are excellent manifestations, declared to be twenty types. One is named 'udbhāsvāra'; and disheveling of hair, a radiant scent, and disarray of clothing are other transformations.

Lamentation, conversation, incoherent talk, repetition, denial, excessive messages, pretense, advice, commands, and indirect references—these are specific types of verbal manifestations.
